mnemonics.title: HTTL - HashMap Time Too Low
mnemonics.content: HashMap: Hash (key) → bucket index → traverse (optional) → locate. O(1) average time. Remember: HashMap provides fast lookup, TreeMap provides sorting.

mnemonics.title: N O K V - No Original Keys, Values
mnemonics.content: Hashtable: No null keys/values. Original (legacy), Synchronized. Remember: Hashtable = No nulls, Synchronized, Legacy.

mnemonics.title: TREE - Tree Red-Black Enables Ordered Retrieval
mnemonics.content: TreeMap uses Red-Black tree for maintaining sorted order of keys. Operations are O(log n). Remember: TREE = Red-Black tree ensures sorted order.

mnemonics.title: L F S - Linked list For Small buckets
mnemonics.content: Java HashMap uses LinkedList for small buckets (<8), converts to Tree for large buckets. Remember: LF8 = LinkedList For buckets < 8.

acronyms.usage: LinkedHashMap with accessOrder=true implements LRU cache by overriding removeEldestEntry() to evict oldest entries when size exceeds limit.
Thrown when map structurally modified during iteration except via iterator.remove(). Use Iterator or ConcurrentHashMap for safe concurrent modification.
LinkedHashMap maintains insertion order by default (not sorted), useful for FIFO processing of entries as they were added.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine an array of boxes (buckets). Each box can hold multiple key-value pairs in a linked list (or tree for many items). Hash function decides which box gets the key.
Draw an inverted tree with root at top. Left subtree has smaller keys, right has larger. Red-black coloring ensures balanced height for O(log n) operations.
Picture boxes connected by strings both forward and backward, showing insertion order. New additions connect to the tail of the chain.

analogies.analogy: Think of a dictionary book - you don't scan every page, you use the letter tabs (hash function) to jump to the right section instantly.
Like a phonebook sorted alphabetically - finding a name takes you through a balanced search tree of comparisons.
Like a playlist with 'Add to Queue' feature - songs play in the order they were added, not alphabetically.
Each entry is like a tag on a locker: the tag shows the locker number (key) and what's inside (value). You can read, change, or remove both.
